characters.name: Our Lady of Peace (Virgin Mary or allegorical Peace figure)

event: Our Lady of Peace Appearing to a Knight
symbols: olive branch held by the lady — peace, reconciliation, and the end of conflict
knight in armor kneeling — the warrior surrendering to divine peace, strength bowing to grace
radiant light surrounding the vision — divine glory and supernatural revelation
angels surrounding the lady — the heavenly host attending the Queen of Peace
white robes — purity, holiness, and the garments of heaven
clasped hands in prayer — the knight's posture of supplication and surrender to the vision
description: A luminous female figure in flowing white robes stands elevated, surrounded by a host of small angels or cherubim with wings. She holds what appears to be an olive branch and extends her other hand in blessing. Before her, a young knight in full plate armor kneels with hands clasped in prayer, gazing up at the apparition. Beams of radiant light emanate from the central figure, illuminating the scene. The background shows stone architecture with steps. The overall atmosphere is ethereal and transcendent.
theological_description: De Morgan's Our Lady of Peace presents a vision of the Blessed Virgin as the bringer of peace — a title rooted in her role as the mother of the Prince of Peace (Isaiah 9:6). The olive branch she holds recalls the dove returning to Noah's ark with an olive leaf, signaling the end of God's judgment and the restoration of peace between heaven and earth (Genesis 8:11). The knight kneeling before her represents the spiritual reality that true peace comes not through arms but through surrender to divine grace. Jesus declared: 'Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you' (John 14:27). Mary herself is intimately connected to this peace — at the Annunciation she consented to bear the one through whom God would reconcile 'to himself all things, whether on earth or in heaven, making peace by the blood of his cross' (Colossians 1:20). The surrounding angels recall the heavenly proclamation at Christ's birth: 'Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased' (Luke 2:14). The warrior laying down his strength before the gentle vision embodies Isaiah's eschatological promise that swords shall be beaten into plowshares (Isaiah 2:4).

scripture_refs.reason: The Prince of Peace whose mother is honored as Our Lady of Peace in this vision
The angelic proclamation of peace on earth — the angels surrounding the lady echo this heavenly chorus
Christ's gift of peace unlike the world's peace — the knight surrendering worldly arms for divine peace
The olive branch of peace after the Flood — the lady holds the ancient symbol of God's reconciliation
Swords beaten into plowshares — the knight kneeling before peace rather than wielding arms
